reasons: Angela can attach to Sora before or during his dive, giving him a shield while he commits into close-range combat.
Angela's remote attach fits Sora's EXP-lane or backline-dive pattern because she does not need to physically walk with him before the fight starts.

proof.summary: Sora wants to enter fights aggressively, while Angela gives him remote shielding and follow-up support during the commit window.
worksBestWhen: Angela saves Heartguard for Sora's real engage.
Sora commits after Angela's channel starts or completes.
failureCases: Angela's channel is interrupted before she attaches.
Sora dives too early and dies before the shield value matters.
Anti-shield or burst damage removes the protection quickly.

reasons: Mathilda can give Sora an extra movement route with Guiding Wind, helping him enter, chase, or exit after committing.
The shield and movement-speed boost reduce the risk of Sora being stranded after using his own mobility tools.

proof.summary: Mathilda enables Sora's dive pattern by giving him a shield, movement speed, and a guided repositioning option.
worksBestWhen: Mathilda stays close enough for Sora to trigger Guiding Wind.
Sora keeps one damage or control skill ready after taking the guided movement.
failureCases: Sora is rooted before he can use the guided movement.
Mathilda uses Guiding Wind for another teammate.
The enemy predicts the guided path and counter-engages.

reasons: Kaja can suppress and drag a priority enemy, giving Sora a clean target to burst or chain with Torrent control.
Sora can follow Divine Judgment with form-based damage or crowd control before the target fully resets position.

proof.summary: Kaja locks one enemy with suppression, while Sora follows with Thunder burst or Torrent crowd control to finish the pickoff.
worksBestWhen: Kaja catches a squishy or high-value target.
Sora is close enough to follow immediately after Divine Judgment.
failureCases: Kaja catches only a tank or low-priority target.
Sora is too far away to punish the suppression window.
The enemy team counter-engages after Sora commits.

reasons: Atlas can group multiple enemies with Fatal Links, giving Sora a clear area to enter with Torrent control or Thunder follow-up.
Sora benefits from enemies being dragged into one predictable location because his repeated-hit and area-control tools become easier to apply.

proof.summary: Atlas creates the grouped engage, while Sora follows with form-based burst or crowd control on the displaced enemies.
worksBestWhen: Sora waits near enough to follow the drag immediately.
failureCases: Sora arrives after the enemy has already recovered.
The enemy spreads out and prevents a clean Fatal Links setup.

reasons: Floryn can support Sora globally with Bloom, which fits Sora when he dives away from the main formation.
Flower of Hope adds extra shield value when Floryn heals the chosen teammate, improving Sora's extended-fight durability.

proof.summary: Floryn gives Sora remote healing and shield support, helping him survive longer after committing into skirmishes or teamfights.
worksBestWhen: Floryn tracks Sora's engage and saves Bloom for his real commit.
Sora is the main front or dive threat who receives Floryn's shared support value.
failureCases: Sora is burst down before Bloom resolves.
Floryn must use Bloom for another teammate.
Anti-heal or anti-shield effects reduce the sustain value.
